graph_vector_fulltext.message: This Regulation applies to providers and deployers of AI systems established in third countries to the extent that the output produced by those AI systems is intended to be used in the Union. It also captures situations where, due to the digital nature of AI, an operator established in the Union contracts services to an operator established in a third country in relation to an AI system, even if the AI system is not placed on the market, put into service, or used in the Union—so as to prevent circumvention and ensure effective protection of natural persons located in the Union.
graph_vector_fulltext.metric_question: Under what conditions does this Regulation apply to AI system providers and deployers established in third countries?
graph_vector_fulltext.metric_answer: This Regulation applies to providers and deployers of AI systems established in third countries to the extent that the output produced by those AI systems is intended to be used in the Union. It also captures situations where, due to the digital nature of AI, an operator established in the Union contracts services to an operator established in a third country in relation to an AI system, even if the AI system is not placed on the market, put into service, or used in the Union—so as to prevent circumvention and ensure effective protection of natural persons located in the Union.

Content: Text Content:
 irrespective of whether they are established within the Union or in a third country, and to deployers of AI  systems established within the Union. (22) In light of their digital nature, certain AI systems should fall within the scope of this Regulation even when they are  not placed on the market, put into service, or used in the Union. This is the case, for example, where an operator  established in the Union contracts certain services to an operator established in a third country in relation to
----
 AI system resulting from that processing,  without that AI system being placed on the market, put into service or used in the Union. To prevent the  circumvention of this Regulation and to ensure an effective protection of natural persons located in the Union, this  Regulation should also apply to providers and deployers of AI systems that are established in a third country, to the  extent the output produced by those systems is intended to be used in the Union. Nonetheless, to take into account  existing arrangements
----
 placed on the market or put into  service and should provide that documentation to national competent authorities upon request. Such a provider  should be obliged to register the AI system in the EU database established under this Regulation. With a view to  providing further guidance for the practical implementation of the conditions under which the AI systems listed in  an annex to this Regulation are, on an exceptional basis, non-high-risk, the Commission should, after consulting the  Board, provide guidelines specifying that practical implementation,
----
 the providers to comply with the mandatory requirements of  this Regulation should take into account the generally acknowledged state of the art on AI, be proportionate and  effective to meet the objectives of this Regulation. Based on the New Legislative Framework, as clarified in  Commission notice ‘The “Blue Guide” on the implementation of EU product rules 2022’, the general rule is that  more than one legal act of Union harmonisation legislation may be applicable to one product, since the making

chunks.text: irrespective of whether they are established within the Union or in a third country, and to deployers of AI systems established within the Union. (22) In light of their digital nature, certain AI systems should fall within the scope of this Regulation even when they are not placed on the market, put into service, or used in the Union. This is the case, for example, where an operator established in the Union contracts certain services to an operator established in a third country in relation to

AI system resulting from that processing, without that AI system being placed on the market, put into service or used in the Union. To prevent the circumvention of this Regulation and to ensure an effective protection of natural persons located in the Union, this Regulation should also apply to providers and deployers of AI systems that are established in a third country, to the extent the output produced by those systems is intended to be used in the Union. Nonetheless, to take into account existing arrangements

placed on the market or put into service and should provide that documentation to national competent authorities upon request. Such a provider should be obliged to register the AI system in the EU database established under this Regulation. With a view to providing further guidance for the practical implementation of the conditions under which the AI systems listed in an annex to this Regulation are, on an exceptional basis, non-high-risk, the Commission should, after consulting the Board, provide guidelines specifying that practical implementation,

the providers to comply with the mandatory requirements of this Regulation should take into account the generally acknowledged state of the art on AI, be proportionate and effective to meet the objectives of this Regulation. Based on the New Legislative Framework, as clarified in Commission notice ‘The “Blue Guide” on the implementation of EU product rules 2022’, the general rule is that more than one legal act of Union harmonisation legislation may be applicable to one product, since the making
